title: Saturday Recovery Spin at Skov
objective: Flush the legs after the long ride to the cottage - a short, low-pressure spin by feel, or full rest if Friday emptied the tank.
why_now: Friday was a 4-5h point-to-point. Saturday's job is circulation and movement, not load. With both bikes at Skov this can be an easy gravel or road spin on quiet roads; if the legs or back are cooked, it is rest with no debt.

source_context.week_objective: Re-entry and aerobic rebuild after a disrupted, manual-labor weekend

targets.notes: Up to 60 min very easy at 50-62% FTP. Soft pedal, low force, no climbs pushed. This is recovery - if it stops feeling like recovery, go home. Skip entirely and rest if Friday's legs/back say so.

warmup.notes: Start very easy on flat or rolling roads near the cottage.

steps.notes: Easy spin, smooth cadence, enjoy the lake/forest roads. Walk the bike or turn around on any steep pitch rather than loading the legs.

nutrition.post: Keep refueling from Friday - protein plus carbs across the day; you are still topping up from the long ride.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.next_morning_check: Sunday is optional endurance by feel; only ride if the legs and back are genuinely good.

skill_focus.action: Keep torque low and cadence smooth the whole spin.
skill_focus.why: Low-force pedalling flushes the legs and unloads the back after the long ride without adding fatigue.

mental_focus.why: The week's load is already in the bank from Friday; today protects it.
mental_focus.reset: If you start pressing, shift easier or just turn for home.

pre_interval_cues: Recovery or rest - your call from how Friday's legs feel.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Cut it short or go home; rest is a valid choice today.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Legs looser than they started, no added fatigue.
